name: MoarVM new-disp Dispatcher Mechanism
summary: MoarVM's unified dispatch substrate: a recorded dispatch program (guards + delegate + resume) replaces ad-hoc dispatch implementations for method calls, multi-dispatch, role mixin, role composition, and other Raku dispatch kinds. One mechanism handles all dispatch shapes; spesh specialises the dispatcher just like any other code.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**new-disp** is MoarVM's unified dispatch substrate, replacing ad-hoc dispatch implementations for the various Raku dispatch kinds (method calls, multi-dispatch, role mixin, role composition, currying). A dispatcher program — written in NQP (Not Quite Perl, MoarVM's bootstrap subset) — records guards, delegate calls, and resume points; the runtime re-runs the program at every dispatch site.

The design point: rather than bake each dispatch kind into the VM as a special opcode, define a generic dispatcher mechanism and let user code (NQP, ultimately) implement each dispatch flavour as a dispatcher program.

sources.label: The new MoarVM dispatch mechanism is here (jnthn, 2021)

sections.title: Dispatcher programs
sections.content:
A dispatcher program is a sequence of operations:

- **Guards** check that the inputs match the recorded conditions (target type, arity, role, etc.). If a guard fails, the dispatcher re-runs from the top with the new inputs, possibly recording a new program for that case.
- **Delegate** invokes another dispatcher (for multi-stage dispatch — first the multi-dispatch, then the per-candidate method call).
- **Resume** continues execution at a specific point with the dispatched callee.

Spesh sees dispatcher programs as ordinary code and specialises them: the recorded guards become inline-cache-style fast paths; the delegate calls become inlined when possible; the resume points become direct jumps. The dispatch overhead collapses from "call into VM dispatcher logic" to "check a couple of guards and jump to the dispatched body."

sources.label: Raku multiple dispatch with the new MoarVM dispatcher (jnthn, 2021)

sections.title: Design lesson
sections.content:
The new-disp design lesson generalises: when a runtime needs to support many flavours of dispatch (method, multi-dispatch, mixin, currying, type-class resolution), one generic dispatcher mechanism with user-programmable dispatch programs is cleaner than multiple specialised opcodes.

The cost is upfront design effort plus a non-trivial NQP-style dispatch DSL. The benefit is that adding a new dispatch flavour later doesn't require new VM opcodes — it's a new dispatcher program. MoarVM had to refactor substantial existing VM code to migrate to new-disp; for a new VM, designing this in from the start is much cheaper.
